title: Lovely Polly Stewart.
content:
Tune - "Ye're welcome, Charlie Stewart."



I.

        O lovely Polly Stewart!
            O charming Polly Stewart!
        There's not a flower that blooms in May
            That's half so fair as thou art.
        The flower it blaws, it fades and fa's,
            And art can ne'er renew it;
        But worth and truth eternal youth
            Will give to Polly Stewart.

II.

        May he whose arms shall fauld thy charms
            Possess a leal and true heart;
        To him be given to ken the heaven
            He grasps in Polly Stewart.
        O lovely Polly Stewart!
            O charming Polly Stewart!
        There's ne'er a flower that blooms in May
            That's half so sweet as thou art.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Lovely Polly Stewart.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
